data.title: As a practitioner, routing overlaps include Playwright navigation assertions
data.description:
## Description

**What**: When a consideration-level interaction involves user-navigable URL overlaps, the injected AC includes a Playwright navigation assertion. Non-navigable overlaps get standard integration ACs.
**Why**: Ensures that the combined routing table is validated via browser navigation, not just static analysis.
**Scope**:
- IN: Navigable vs non-navigable route classification, Playwright assertion template for navigable overlaps, standard integration AC template for non-navigable, stack-awareness check
- OUT: Classification logic (S1), AC injection mechanism (S2)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, user-navigable URL prefix overlaps produce ACs with Playwright navigation assertions that exercise at least one URL in the overlap zone
  <- Satisfies: "injected AC includes a cross-feature Playwright navigation assertion"
- When this story is complete, non-navigable overlaps (API-only endpoints, webhook receivers, middleware-internal paths) produce standard integration ACs without Playwright assertions
  <- Satisfies: "Non-navigable overlaps receive standard integration ACs"
- When this story is complete, the Playwright AC template is only injected when the project has a web frontend (checked via Playwright availability in project dependencies or commands.test_e2e config presence). Projects without a web stack receive standard integration ACs for all overlaps
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] DSO is stack-agnostic — Playwright is irrelevant for CLI tools, libraries, and backend-only services. The classifier must detect whether Playwright is available before injecting Playwright ACs

## Escalation Policy
Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue that could send implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
